stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The painter Amrita Sher-Gil is one of the most important artists of twentieth-century South Asia.
  • She was born in Budapest, Hungary, in 1913, to a Hungarian mother and a Sikh Indian father.
  • She is best known for her bold and emotionally expressive portraits of rural Indian life.
  • Three Girls (1935) features three young women seated together against an ochre background.
  • One woman wears a red sari, while the other two wear muted greens and grays.
stem: The student wants to introduce Sher-Gil to an audience unfamiliar with the artist. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Known for being bold and emotionally expressive, Amrita Sher-Gil's portraits include Three Girls (1935).
The 1935 painting Three Girls is one example of a portrait by Amrita Sher-Gil.
One painting by Amrita Sher-Gil features three young women, one wearing a red sari and the others wearing muted greens and grays.
One of the most important artists of twentieth-century South Asia, Hungarian-Indian painter Amrita Sher-Gil is best known for her bold, emotionally expressive portraits of rural Indian life.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. By identifying Sher-Gil as an important artist with a particular heritage and by describing the work she's best known for, this choice provides the background information necessary to introduce Sher-Gil to an unfamiliar audience.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice doesn't effectively introduce Sher-Gil. It doesn't include any background information about who Sher-Gil is or where she's from. It simply identifies one of her paintings. Choice B is incorrect. This choice doesn't effectively introduce Sher-Gil. It doesn't include any background information about who Sher-Gil is. It simply identifies one of her paintings. Choice C is incorrect. This choice doesn't effectively introduce Sher-Gil. It doesn't include any background information about who Sher-Gil is. Instead, it describes one of her paintings in detail.
